title: Multidisciplinary pain plan documentation-only
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.
examination.rationale: Supports treatment plan, coping, function and specialist review for multidisciplinary pain plan documentation; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.

source.title: Neuropathic pain in adults: pharmacological management in non-specialist settings

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7 — assessment, treatment planning, referral, and review

treatment_and_management.text: Supports functional pain assessment, complex pain or pain-service review, discharge communication, and clinician-arranged follow-up without autonomous treatment.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Supports complex-pain and pain-service follow-up context for multidisciplinary pain plan documentation; no decision is generated.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports functional pain assessment, complex pain or pain-service review, discharge communication, and clinician-arranged follow-up without autonomous treatment.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Diagnoses, goals, disciplines, medicines, physical and psychological care, responsibilities, risks, patient preferences, measures and review date are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for multidisciplinary pain plan documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
